butterflyfish.description: Butterflyfish rely heavily on live coral and are widely regarded as indicators of healthy reef ecosystems.

surgeonfish.description: Surgeonfish graze on algae, preventing excessive algal growth and maintaining reef balance.

parrotfish.description: Parrotfish help maintain coral reefs by grazing algae and producing coral sand.

angelfish.description: Angelfish are commonly found in biodiverse coral reef habitats.

clownfish.description: Clownfish live symbiotically with sea anemones, indicating stable reef environments.

lionfish.description: Lionfish are venomous predators. In some regions they are invasive and threaten native reef biodiversity.

moray eel.description: Moray eels inhabit reef crevices and contribute to maintaining ecological balance.

reef shark.description: Reef sharks regulate prey populations and indicate a relatively healthy marine ecosystem.

hammerhead shark.description: Hammerhead sharks are important top predators whose presence reflects ecosystem complexity.

sea turtle.description: Sea turtles are protected species that contribute to healthy seagrass beds and coral reef ecosystems.

loggerhead turtle.description: Loggerhead turtles help regulate marine invertebrate populations and are internationally protected.

stingray.description: Stingrays forage on the seabed and help cycle nutrients within benthic habitats.

manta ray.description: Manta rays are large filter feeders whose presence often indicates healthy plankton populations.

octopus.description: Octopuses are highly intelligent predators that occupy important ecological niches.

jellyfish.description: Large jellyfish blooms may indicate changing ocean conditions or ecosystem imbalance.

dolphin.description: Dolphins are apex predators and often indicate productive marine ecosystems.

whale.description: Whales play a vital role in nutrient cycling and contribute significantly to ocean ecosystem health.

starfish.description: Sea stars contribute to maintaining ecological balance within reef and benthic communities.

sea urchin.description: Sea urchins help control algae, but population explosions may damage coral reefs.

seahorse.description: Seahorses depend on healthy seagrass and coral habitats and are sensitive to habitat degradation.
